arts.ttl: OFFICIAL: Chelsea recall Trevoh Chalobah from Crystal Palace loan
arts.sum: Chelsea have officially recalled Trevoh Chalobah from his loan spell at Crystal Palace . OFFICIAL: Chelsea recall Trevoh Chalobah from Crystal Palace loan

arts.ttl: Chelsea's efforts to sign Marc Guehi in the balance after Trevoh Chalobah was recalled from Crystal Palace loan spell - with the Eagles not looking to sign a replacement for the defender
arts.sum: SAMI MOKBEL: Centre back Chalobah, who signed a season-long loan deal with the Selhurst Park club in the summer, has returned to Stamford Bridge after Chelsea activated a recall clause.
Crystal Palace can confirm that Chelsea have activated an option to recall Trevoh Chalobah from his loan spell.

arts.ttl: Palace for Life's Soccer Schools return this February half-term!
arts.sum: Palace for Life's popular Soccer Schools programme is back once again, filling young footballers' school holidays with opportunities to develop their skills and meet new friends.

arts.ttl: Leicester City transfer news: Foxes 'consider signing' for key position amid major injury concern
arts.sum: Leicester City are reportedly considering a January move for a new goalkeeper as they wait for an update on the groin injury of Mads Hermansen.

arts.ttl: Everton, Leicester City 'put off' swoop for Premier League defender by eye-catching asking price
arts.sum: Southampton are reportedly demanding £7m for the services of Kyle Walker-Peters, with Everton and Leicester currently unwilling to pay such a fee for the defender.

arts.ttl: Joe Hart says striker Tottenham signed for just £9m was actually better than Jamie Vardy
arts.sum: Joe Hart has been ranking some of the best strikers of the Premier League era. Joe Hart played with and against some of the best...

arts.ttl: Report: Crystal Palace could bid for £3m defender compared to Rudiger after Chelsea recall Chalobah
Exclusive: How Crystal Palace have reacted as Chelsea recall Trevoh Chalobah, plans now being made
Leicester City rivals hit by sudden player recall as frustration clear
arts.sum: Chelsea’s boss, the former Leicester manager Enzo Maresca, gave no hint of the recall when asked about Chalobah earlier this week, saying: “He is a Palace player. Palace boss Oliver Glasner admitted his frustration over the Chalobah situation in his pre-match press conference.
